text.title: Django Testing with pytest — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every django-testing application must obey.

rule.statement: Configure pytest-django via pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] with DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE, addopts including --strict-markers, and testpaths.
rule.rationale: pyproject-as-single-source-of-truth: eliminates pytest.ini/setup.cfg duplication and keeps lint+test config co-located.

rule.statement: Use @pytest.mark.django_db (savepoint rollback) by default. Use @pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True) only when code calls on_commit or raw transactions.
rule.rationale: Savepoints are 5–10x faster than truncate-after-test; reserve transactional mode for code paths that actually depend on commit semantics.

rule.statement: Put shared fixtures in tests/conftest.py: api_client, authed_client, staff_client, and factory registrations. Never instantiate APIClient inside individual test functions.
rule.rationale: Centralised fixtures give one canonical client construction and let force_authenticate stay decoupled from the auth backend.

rule.statement: Create exactly one Factory Boy factory per model, registered via pytest_factoryboy.register. Use SubFactory for relationships, Faker for realistic data, LazyAttribute for derived fields, LazyFunction for independent computed values.
rule.rationale: Single factory prevents divergent defaults; pytest_factoryboy auto-creates instance and factory fixtures, eliminating manual fixture boilerplate.

rule.statement: Use DRF's APIClient with client.force_authenticate(user=user) for authed tests. Do not use credentials(HTTP_AUTHORIZATION=...) in view tests.
rule.rationale: force_authenticate bypasses token validation and tests the view logic only — switching auth backends does not break view tests.

rule.statement: Assert status code first, then assert specific response.data fields. Always use format='json'. Never rely solely on status_code == 200.
rule.rationale: A 200 with missing fields is a bug; asserting individual fields catches serializer drift and field renames.

rule.statement: Use @pytest.mark.parametrize to express permission matrices: (client_fixture, expected_status). One parametrized test replaces N nearly-identical tests.
rule.rationale: Combinatorial permission logic (roles × methods) is explicit and maintainable as a parametrize table; adding a role is one tuple, not a new function.
